name: Romulan Lucernae
recipeInstructions:
1. One tangerine per person, with a couple extra for practice.
2. Cut through the rind of the tangerine around the middle, closer to the stem end than the bottom.
3. Gently loosen the skin from the sections and carefully remove the top (the half including the stem end).
4. Gently remove the sections from the bottom half, leaving some pithy center attached to the bottom shell.
5. Press the pithy strands together to form a wick about 1/4 to 1/2 inch long.
6. Place a spoonful of oil in the bottom half and swirl it around to coat the inside of the shell.
7. With the whole inside of the shell oil-coated, add or remove oil to leave a slight puddle in the bottom of the shell about 1/16 inch deep.
8. Light the wick. This may require several tries before it stays lit.
9. Take the top half and cut a 1-inch hole in the center for air circulation when setting the top back on the bottom half.
10. Oil the inside of the top half, then wipe it dry to allow more light to shine through.
11. Place the lamp on top of the votive cup; it is ready.
